data.title: Stanford releases 2026 AI Index report: AI's electricity demand has reached half that of Bitcoin mining.
data.description: A Stanford University report, the "2026 AI Index Report," reveals that the overall electricity demand of current AI systems is approaching half the scale of Bitcoin mining and nearing the national electricity consumption levels of Switzerland or Austria. The report also points out that the gap in AI model capabilities between China and the US is narrowing. In 2025, the US is projected to launch approximately 50 representative models, while China will have around 30. Models from institutions such as Anthropic, xAI, Google, OpenAI, Alibaba, and DeepSeek are considered to be in the same capability tier. Furthermore, China leads in AI paper citations (20.6%) and patent numbers (74.2%), while US private AI investment reaches $285.9 billion, significantly higher than China's $12.4 billion.

data.title: The White House is accelerating the passage of the crypto market structure bill, possibly related to the midterm election window.
data.description: With the US midterm elections approaching, the White House is accelerating the push for a crypto market structure bill to alleviate the long-standing dispute between the banking and crypto industries. Reports indicate that several figures, including Treasury Secretary Scott Bessant, White House crypto advisor Patrick Witt, and other policymakers, have recently publicly called for the bill's advancement. The Council of Economic Advisers has also released a report addressing the banking industry's concerns about the crypto sector. Analysts suggest that the current timeline may be a crucial window for advancing this legislation, but the bill's successful passage remains uncertain. (The Hill)

data.title: White House Urges Resolution of Banking and Crypto Industry Dispute
data.description:
The White House is reportedly eager to resolve the ongoing dispute between the banking and cryptocurrency sectors, which has hindered Senate negotiations on related legislation since January. According to BlockBeats, key officials within and outside the U.S. government, including Treasury Secretary Scott Besant, White House crypto advisor Patrick Witt, and former AI and crypto affairs head David Sachs, have recently advocated for the passage of the bill. Additionally, the Council of Economic Advisers has released a report countering concerns raised by the banking industry.

However, it remains uncertain whether the White House's efforts will be sufficient to overcome the challenges facing the legislation, which extend beyond the narrow industry dispute that has dominated discussions over the past three months. Christopher Niebuhr, a senior research analyst at Beacon Policy Advisors, noted in an interview, "From a timing perspective, their judgment is reasonable—if Congress has an opportunity to push the market structure bill through, now is the time."

data.title: STOCKS | Hong Kong Solid-State Battery Sector Sees Gains
data.description: The solid-state battery sector in Hong Kong's stock market experienced a notable rise. According to Jin10, Longpan Technology (02465.HK) saw an increase of nearly 20%, while Ganfeng Lithium (01772.HK) rose by 4.4%, and Tianqi Lithium (09696.HK) gained 4%. These movements reflect growing investor interest in the sector.

data.title: Iran Warns U.S. Blockade of Strait of Hormuz Could End Ceasefire
data.description: Iran has issued a warning that a potential U.S. blockade of the Strait of Hormuz could lead to the collapse of the current ceasefire between the two nations. According to NS3.AI, Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Baghaei described the possible blockade as a provocative action that might nullify the existing truce.

data.title: Clippers vs. Warriors NBA Play-In Game Sparks Significant Betting Activity
data.description:
A notable betting event has emerged in the Polymarket prediction market surrounding the NBA play-in game between the Los Angeles Clippers and the Golden State Warriors. According to Odaily, an account has reportedly profited over $1.3 million by purchasing $500,000 worth of bets on the Clippers defeating the Warriors, with an average opening price of 67.6 cents.

The NBA play-in game between the Clippers and Warriors is scheduled to begin at 10 a.m. UTC+8 today. The winner of this matchup will face the Phoenix Suns for the final playoff spot in the Western Conference.

data.title: Circle Introduces New Solution for High-Frequency USDC Payments Across Blockchains
data.description:
Circle has unveiled a new solution aimed at facilitating high-frequency USDC payments across blockchains. According to ChainCatcher, the new approach leverages the Cross-Chain Transfer Protocol (CCTP), allowing local fulfillment parties to advance payments on the recipient's designated chain, with subsequent cross-chain settlement handled by the platform.

This model is designed to alleviate the operational burden associated with individual cross-chain transfers, making it suitable for platforms that process large volumes of payments daily. Unlike the traditional CCTP process, which involves the destruction and minting of USDC for each transaction, the new solution supports batch settlements, reducing the number of source chain destructions and eliminating the need for signature infrastructure on the target chain. Circle has also demonstrated the process using the Arc Testnet and Ethereum Sepolia.

data.title: Binance's USD1 holding subsidy program will be extended to May 15th, with an additional $15 million WLFI token prize pool.
data.description: Binance officially announced on X that the USD1 holding rewards event will be extended to 8:00 AM Beijing time on May 15th. Eligible USD1 holders will share a WLFI rewards pool worth $15 million, with rewards still being distributed every Friday.

data.title: Solv Protocol and Utexo Collaborate on Bitcoin Native Yield Solution
data.description:
Solv Protocol has participated as a strategic angel investor in Utexo's $7.5 million seed funding round, led by Tether. According to Foresight News, the two entities have formed a deep strategic integration to jointly launch a Bitcoin native yield solution.

Utilizing the RGB protocol and Lightning Network, Solv aims to achieve true atomic-level BTC and USDT exchanges without the need for wrapped assets, cross-chain bridges, or custodianship, ensuring complete self-custody, privacy protection, and sub-second settlement.

This integration directly aligns with Tether's plan, announced in August 2025, to deploy USDT natively on RGB-compatible Lightning rails, providing essential infrastructure for the commercialization of Bitcoin native stablecoins.

Solv stated, "Bitcoin native yields have long been diluted by wrapped assets and intermediaries. Through Utexo, we draw a clear line: true yields must be directly built on native Bitcoin rails, prioritizing security, privacy, and settlement integrity at an institutional scale."

data.title: Expert Warns U.S. Risks Lagging in Crypto Competition
data.description: Patrick Witt has cautioned that the United States may fall behind in the global cryptocurrency competition if it does not establish a sustainable market structure framework. According to NS3.AI, Witt emphasized the importance of developing a robust regulatory environment to ensure the U.S. remains competitive in the rapidly evolving crypto landscape. Without such a framework, the country risks losing its edge in the digital asset market, potentially impacting its economic and technological leadership.

data.title: Justin Sun criticized the WLFI unlocking proposal, calling it "world tyranny," not "world free finance."
data.description: In response to WLFI's token unlocking proposal released yesterday, Justin Sun again criticized it, stating, "This is 'world tyranny,' not 'world free finance.' This proposal is packaged as a 'governance alignment signal' and a 'long-term commitment,' but peeled back the layers, it's one of the most absurd governance scams I've ever seen. I will explain point by point." Sun further explained that the proposal has five major controversial points, including: 1. Punishment for opposition—a classic form of coercion; 2. Selective freezing of voters; 3. All actual power being stolen by anonymous individuals; 4. Voters must use their real names, while the rulers remain anonymous—even worse than tyranny; 5. A blatant violation of billions of dollars in property rights. Sun concluded, "I urge all WLFI holders to recognize the true nature of this proposal, express their opposition through all public channels, and reserve all rights to pursue legal action." Recommended reading: Good news, your WLFI is about to be unlocked; bad news, you'll have to wait for Trump's retirement.

data.title: SOL Spot ETFs See $5.36 Million Net Inflows, Led by Bitwise
data.description: U.S. SOL spot ETFs experienced net inflows totaling $5.3641 million yesterday. According to NS3.AI, data from SoSoValue highlighted that Bitwise's Solana Staking ETF was at the forefront, contributing $3.21 million to the inflows. The total net asset value for SOL spot ETFs reached $835 million.

data.title: Whale Trader Closes $398 Million Crypto Positions for $68.47 Million Profit
data.description: A major trader on Hyperliquid has closed significant long positions in Ethereum (ETH) and Bitcoin (BTC), totaling approximately $398 million. According to ChainCatcher, the trader achieved a cumulative profit of around $68.47 million. The positions were opened between February and March across four addresses, involving 120,000 ETH and 1,500 BTC. Following a substantial rebound in the crypto market, the trader began closing these positions in batches. The ETH positions were opened at prices ranging from $2,000 to $2,150 and closed near $2,390, while the BTC positions were opened at approximately $68,420 and closed near $74,700.

data.title: Kalshi plans to launch a "parent portal" feature to strengthen restrictions on minors' use of the app.
data.description: Tarek Mansour, CEO of prediction market platform Kalshi, stated that the company plans to launch a "parent portal" feature, allowing parents to submit their identity information to verify whether their children are using their identity to access the platform. In addition, Kalshi will introduce a selfie verification mechanism, using facial recognition to further confirm user identity, thereby strengthening age restrictions and compliance management to prevent minors from using related services. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Crypto stocks closed higher across the board in the US market, with ALTS rising over 11.46%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, at the close of trading on the US stock market, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.15%, the S&P 500 rose 0.8%, and the Nasdaq Composite rose 1.59%. The crypto sector collectively rallied, with ALTS rising over 11.46%, HOOD up 10.41%, ABTC up 6.8%, COIN up 6.23%, and MSTR up 4.46%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: Gold rose to $4,822, while silver gained over 1%.
data.description: According to Gate data, gold prices rose to $4822.36 per ounce, a daily increase of 0.65%; silver was at $79.815 per ounce, a daily increase of 1.11%. In terms of volatility, the BVIX was at 44.80, up 0.04%; the EVIX was at 66.54, up 1.51%. In the forex market, USD/CNH was at 6.81290 and USD/JPY was at 158.782, both slightly lower. Globally, the Euro Stoxx 50 fell 0.72%, the FTSE 100 rose 0.07%, and the DAX 40 rose 0.24%. In commodities, WTI crude oil was at $90.82 per barrel, down 0.80%; Brent crude oil was at $96.40 per barrel, down 0.52%.

data.title: Tether Increases Bitcoin Holdings with Recent Acquisition
data.description: Tether has recently transferred 951 BTC to its Bitcoin reserve address, according to CoinDesk. This move increases Tether's total Bitcoin holdings to 97,141 BTC. The address used for this transaction is labeled as 'Tether: BTC Reserve' and aligns with the address previously confirmed by Tether CEO Paolo Ardoino. This acquisition is part of Tether's ongoing strategy since 2023 to allocate up to 15% of its realized operating profits into Bitcoin.
